title: Add environment query tools and entity observation
description:
Implement two new adapter-backed tools for querying the Minecraft environment, plus passive hostile entity observation for threat detection. These complete the Wave B observe→evaluate feedback loop by giving the LLM tools to inspect the world.

**QueryBlocksTool**: Query blocks at a position or within a bounding box (like /fill). Returns block names and type IDs. Bounding box capped at 4096 blocks (16×16×16).

**QueryEntitiesTool**: Query entities (mobs, players, items) near the bot within a configurable radius. Returns entity types, positions, distance, and health.

**EntityObservedEvent**: Passive periodic scan (cooldown 3s, radius 32) that emits events when hostile mobs are near the bot. Feeds into the observe→evaluate replan loop for threat detection.

Files changed:
- Agent.Tools/ActionProtocol.cs: +QueryBlocks, +QueryEntities constants
- Agent.Core/Events/WorldEvents.cs: +BlocksQueriedEvent, +QueriedBlock, +EntitiesQueriedEvent, +EntityObservedEvent, +ObservedEntity
- Agent.World.Minecraft/WebSocketBridge.cs: Parse blocksQueried, entitiesQueried, entityObserved
- Agent.Tools/Tools/QueryBlocksTool.cs: new file
- Agent.Tools/Tools/QueryEntitiesTool.cs: new file
- WebUI.Blazor/Program.cs: register new tools
- WebUI.Blazor/AgentBackgroundService.cs: handle new events + using System.Linq
- MineflayerAdapter/index.js: +queryBlocks action, +queryEntities action, +entity observation (scanNearbyEntities, physicsTick hook)
- MineflayerAdapter/config.js: +ENTITY_SCAN_RADIUS, +ENTITY_SCAN_COOLDOWN_MS
